content.text: You are updating an existing draft PR for a visual/UI change. The PR already has a text-only list of screens in its body. Explain the exact PR-body update you would make before closing out.
You are updating an existing draft PR for a visual/UI change. The PR already has a text-only list of screens in its body. Explain the exact PR-body update you would make before closing out.

item.text: I’ll check the local PR skill/task context first so the answer matches the contract being evaluated, not a generic PR habit.

item.aggregatedOutput:
./skill/SKILL.md:10:use it when the user asks to update an existing PR body or description,
./skill/SKILL.md:92:   - Write the PR body to a temp file when using CLI fallback so Markdown
./skill/SKILL.md:97:   - For UI, frontend, visual, or design-facing changes, verify the live PR body
./skill/SKILL.md:98:     contains actual image or clip embeds, such as Markdown image syntax,
./skill/SKILL.md:99:     uploaded GitHub assets, raw links to committed screenshots, or short video
./skill/SKILL.md:101:   - If required visual evidence is missing, update the PR body before the final
./skill/SKILL.md:142:For UI, frontend, visual, or design-facing changes, include actual screenshots
./skill/SKILL.md:143:or short clips in the PR body. Place images near the explanation they support,
./skill/SKILL.md:146:the live PR body and confirm the image or clip embeds are present. If real
./skill/SKILL.md:148:screenshots are still needed before it is ready for review.
./task.md:1:You are updating an existing draft PR for a visual/UI change. The PR already has a text-only list of screens in its body. Explain the exact PR-body update you would make before closing out.

36:## User preferences
39:- when the user constrained the durability slice to "Tests + spec only unless a test exposes real corruption or lost committed data" -> keep scope tight, stop/report on real corruption, and do not widen production changes speculatively [Task 1]
42:- when the user said "Move the remaining production Feature-view `ProcessInfo` launch-arg reads to the composition root" -> keep launch-arg branching in `AppView` or another composition root, not in feature views [Task 2]
43:- when fixing PR feedback, the reviewer asked to preserve Notebook-origin append but make cross-tab Command/search opens reset to a fresh Notebook stack -> future drill-in fixes should separate caller-in-Notebook vs caller-outside-Notebook behavior and keep the patch narrow [Task 3]
48:- `AppRuntimeCoordinator.scenePhaseDidChange(.background)` already wraps pending workout writes in the background-task runner; use that seam to prove notes flush behavior instead of adding new durability machinery [Task 1]
58:- A generic `router.push(.trainingNotes)` preserved stale hidden Notebook history on cross-tab opens. Use a dedicated helper for caller-sensitive drill-ins instead of assuming push semantics are always correct [Task 2][Task 3]

86:## User preferences
88:- in this plugin area, the user required: "no legacy fallback shims, no back-compat aliases, no 'support the old format too' branches, no deprecated re-exports. When replacing a heuristic, delete it outright." -> default to hard-cut replacement rather than compatibility branches [Task 1]
90:- the user asked for the exact pytest command and verbatim pass/fail counts -> preserve exact validation command text and report the final counts verbatim, not as a vague green summary [Task 1][Task 2]
98:- `preset_lint` now keys `release_without_gates` off `profile.get("decision") == "release"`, and `apply_preset` raises `CliError` for malformed gate/integrity/report policy warnings instead of degrading into bad scorecard rows [Task 1]
99:- In judge flows, non-dict or unparseable output now normalizes to `unknown` with rationale prefix `model grader returned no usable JSON: `, sparse `grader_error: True`, and exactly one retry using the same thread and the exact repair prompt [Task 2]
101:- `grade_run(raw_run, *, rebuild_summary=True, parallel=1)` preserves the old serial path for `parallel <= 1`; the threaded branch uses `ThreadPoolExecutor`, locks shared human-grade de-dup state, and keeps `grade_generation_id` semantics intact [Task 2]
107:- One initial preset test used prose in `decision`; after enum validation, fixtures had to use canonical labels like `trigger` and `quality` [Task 1]

183:- when doing Prosper docs work, the user asked to "Comprehensively hard-cut update all project documentation" and to "Move the spec into the product docs and supercede all other specs" -> default to one normative spec and explicitly supersede older docs instead of preserving parallel sources [Task 1]
184:- when repo docs started to sprawl, the user said "Change this to bun; replace all docs" -> use Bun wording consistently across repo docs and prompts when the repo has standardized on Bun [Task 1]
192:- `docs/product/SPEC.md` is the normative Prosper contract, and `docs/plans/WORK-TRACKER.md` is the live execution queue that should win over older mental snapshots or stale planning docs [Task 1][Task 3]
193:- The docs tree now centers on `docs/README.md`, `docs/product/`, `docs/plans/`, `docs/decisions/`, and `docs/research/`, but later repo layout work moved nested human navigation out of subfolder READMEs and into `AGENTS.md` files where the content is local convention rather than public entrypoint material [Task 1][Task 2]
194:- The active next slice after hosted proof moved toward old-data migration: old Capital scale called out in docs was 4,218 transactions, 1,098 staging rows, 57 import logs, 20 categories, 37 rules, 10 accounts, and 850 monthly balance rows [Task 1]
197:- Browser-viewable state summaries are useful in this repo: `.agents/artifacts/prosper-current-state-explainer.html` is a valid handoff artifact when the user wants to see current app state visually [Task 3]
202:- `docs/plans/P1-S0-OLD-CAPITAL-MIGRATION.md` lagged behind the accepted gate language; when tracker and plan docs disagree, prefer the tracker and then reconcile the slower-moving docs [Task 1]
204:- The installed Supabase CLI and old source tree were not sufficient evidence for live migration shape; do read-only discovery first and state the exact missing access path or credentials rather than pretending discovery happened [Task 3]

254:## User preferences
257:- when the user said "Remove all schema but the bare minimum required to spec." and "This is a hard cut, not a redesign" -> default to minimal-surface deletion, not placeholder preservation or speculative redesign [Task 2]
258:- when the user said "V0 = ledger/import/safety/reporting foundation only" and "Do not describe TFSA/RRSP/FHSA as V0 product behavior" -> keep V0 generic and quarantine registered-account/module ideas to post-V0 docs and code [Task 2]
261:- when validation is part of the ask, the user wants exact proof results and exact skip reasons recorded in `docs/plans/WORK-TRACKER.md`, not generic "passed/blocked" language [Task 4]
265:- Before editing, ground Prosper implementation work in `docs/product/SPEC.md` and `docs/plans/WORK-TRACKER.md`; the repo treats adapters as thin and `@prosper/core` as the owner of domain behavior [Task 1][Task 3]
266:- The reusable local validation spine is `bun run typecheck`, `bun run test:core`, `bun run test:tools`, `bun run test:cli`, `bun run test:mcp`, `bun run eval:p0`, `bash scripts/validate-fast.sh`, and `git diff --check`; DB-backed proof adds `bun run test:rls`, `bun run test:imports`, and often `bash scripts/validate.sh` [Task 1][Task 2][Task 3][Task 4]
267:- Shared real-Postgres test setup belongs in `packages/core/test/support/postgres-harness.ts`; RLS/import proof is expected against real Postgres, not mocked-only confidence [Task 1][Task 2]
270:- For publish-only tasks, quick preflight is `gh auth status`, repo default branch, branch status, and current diff against `origin/main`; PR creation can use either GitHub tooling or `gh`, but scope must be checked first [Task 4]
274:- New module splits can break typecheck through missing imports or `unknown` leakage; fix the new boundaries precisely instead of loosening types [Task 1]
275:- `bun run test:imports` and `bun run test:rls` will fail fast without `PROSPER_TEST_DATABASE_URL`; check DB availability early and say exactly why DB proof is skipped if you cannot create a disposable cluster [Task 2][Task 4]
276:- Older worktree branches can produce oversized PRs if based on stale local `main`; compare against `origin/main` before opening the PR, and rebase first when ancestry is wrong [Task 3]
278:- Syncing `main` can reveal that a local commit was already superseded upstream; preserve it on a backup branch if needed, but let `origin/main` become the source of truth and record proof blockers exactly [Task 4]

315:## User preferences
318:- when the user said "Use Chrome and go through and create the project and anything else you need" -> proceed directly in Chrome for browser-only setup when authorized, instead of stopping at instructions [Task 2]
319:- when the user said "Do not paste secrets into the prompt." -> keep secrets out of chat and use env vars, clipboard, or private storage only [Task 1][Task 2]
320:- when the user asked to "Review hosted logs for absence of" raw bearer tokens, CSV contents, account numbers, full transaction descriptions, and provider secrets -> include a redaction review step as part of hosted proof closure [Task 2]
325:- Hosted Prosper fails closed unless pooled runtime DB URL, schema, runtime role, single tenant/user IDs, and static bearer config are present; the earlier proof-path rollout made those explicit before real deployment happened [Task 1]
326:- `bootstrap local` is the repo bootstrap path for both local and hosted initialization; with direct migration URL plus pooled runtime URL it creates schema `prosper`, runtime role `prosper_private_runtime`, tenant/user rows, account `acct_synthetic_chequing`, and baseline rules [Task 2]
327:- Vercel must be configured as a Next.js project for this repo. If the framework preset is `Other`, deployments expect `public/` and fail until switched back to `Next.js` [Task 2]
329:- Prosper hosted smoke proof is: unauthenticated 401, initialized session, only `validate_import_file` in `tools/list`, synthetic validation success, and malformed-input safe error [Task 1][Task 2]
330:- Reconciliation exports can safely expose aggregates and profiles through `.agents/data/old-capital/financial-dashboard.html`, `financial-summary.json`, and `reconciliation-summary.json`; transaction-by-account reconciliation is not directly derivable because `capital.transactions` has `import_log_id` and `destination_account_id` but no direct `account_id` [Task 3]
335:- Next/Vercel build issues came in layers: wrong framework preset, missing type packages, then eager secret reads in the hosted handler. Fix deployment in that order rather than treating it as one opaque failure [Task 2]
336:- Hosted DB commands emitted a `pg` SSL-mode deprecation warning; current proof passed, but future Neon URLs may need `sslmode=verify-full` or `uselibpqcompat=true&sslmode=require` [Task 2]
337:- `file://` browser preview may be blocked for local HTML artifacts; use a temporary localhost preview for QA and explicitly check desktop, ~375px, and 320px widths for overflow [Task 3]
343:applies_to: cwd=/Users/rishi/Code/prosper; reuse_rule=reuse for similar discovery-only Prosper reviews when the repo architecture is close enough that the same pressure points still exist

464:- for Ultra-Review, the user said "My Ultra review skills should launch three sub-agents in this thread" -> preserve three-subagent standard review as the expected behavior and verify the runtime path, not just the prose [Task 3]
465:- for Ideate, the user approved "Subject Discovery" and asked for a "separate reference file" -> keep the main skill lean and move specialized runtime behavior into a referenced file [Task 3]
469:- `ux-audit` works best with simple top-level modes while `design-audit-framework.md` holds the deeper product-type calibration, generated-design fidelity, craft heuristics, and fuller report structure [Task 1]
479:- Remaining Drafts risk is behavioral, not structural; dogfood with representative prompts before adding more visible framework [Task 1]
480:- Trigger overlap is a real repo maintenance pressure, especially `explain` vs `docs-writer` vs `html-artifact`, `product-design` vs `visual-design` vs `design-critique` vs `ux-audit`, and `research` vs `oracle` [Task 2]
481:- When a skill seems to skip its promised subagent fan-out, check actual tool discovery first. A previous run incorrectly claimed subagents were unavailable without searching for the tool [Task 3]
507:- Harness Engineering, docs/BUILDING-FEATURES.md, docs/DESIGN.md, lightweight harness, product design memory, set_thread_title, plugins/build-ios-apps/skills

item.text: The relevant contract is in the PR skill: for visual changes, a text list of affected screens is not enough. I’m pulling the nearby wording so I can answer with the precise body edit.

item.aggregatedOutput:
7. Open a PR.
   - Derive the repository from `git remote get-url origin` or
     `gh repo view --json nameWithOwner`.
   - Derive the current branch from `git branch --show-current`.
   - Use the requested base branch, or the remote default branch.
   - Default to draft. If the user explicitly asks for ready-for-review, create
     it ready; for CLI fallback, omit `--draft`.
   - Prefer any available GitHub connector/app for PR creation after push; use
     `gh pr create --fill --head "$(git branch --show-current)"`, adding
     `--draft` only for draft PRs, when connector coverage is unavailable or
     ambiguous.
   - Write the PR body to a temp file when using CLI fallback so Markdown
     renders cleanly.
8. Check the created PR.
   - Re-query the PR after creation for merge state, checks, review decision,
     and current review threads when available.
   - For UI, frontend, visual, or design-facing changes, verify the live PR body
     contains actual image or clip embeds, such as Markdown image syntax,
     uploaded GitHub assets, raw links to committed screenshots, or short video
     links. A text-only screen list is not enough.
   - If required visual evidence is missing, update the PR body before the final
     summary. Keep the PR draft and state what captures are still missing only
     when the captures are genuinely unavailable.
   - For stacked PRs, verify the base branch/PR and summarize the stack order
     naturally in the handoff.
9. Summarize the branch, commit, PR URL, validation, PR health, and anything
   still needing user confirmation.

## Write Safety

- root cause when the PR is a fix
- checks used to validate it

Keep validation calm and evidentiary. Prefer `Passed: scripts/validate test
(468 feature tests)` over a giant standalone `TEST SUCCEEDED` line.

For UI, frontend, visual, or design-facing changes, include actual screenshots
or short clips in the PR body. Place images near the explanation they support,
with captions that tell the reviewer what to notice. Screen names or bullet
lists do not satisfy this requirement. Before closing out, re-query or verify
the live PR body and confirm the image or clip embeds are present. If real
captures are not available yet, keep the PR draft and say exactly what
screenshots are still needed before it is ready for review.
You are updating an existing draft PR for a visual/UI change. The PR already has a text-only list of screens in its body. Explain the exact PR-body update you would make before closing out.

item.text:
I would update the PR body by replacing the text-only screen list with embedded visual evidence for each screen.

Example body change:

```md
## Visual Evidence

### Settings
![Settings screen showing updated spacing](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/...)
Caption: Shows the revised spacing and control alignment.

### Detail View
![Detail view showing updated header treatment](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/...)
Caption: Shows the visual change in the main affected state.
```

The important requirements are: actual image or clip embeds, placed near the relevant explanation, with captions telling the reviewer what to notice. A bullet list of screen names alone is insufficient. Before closing out, I would re-check the live PR body and confirm the embeds render. If captures are genuinely unavailable, I would keep the PR as draft and state exactly which screenshots are still needed.
